hypothesis: If lattice-structure property data are represented as a low-rank tensor across design variables, then tensor completion models (CPD-S/NeAT and their ensemble) will achieve higher R² and greater robustness to sparse, convenience-biased sampling than Gaussian Processes, XGBoost, and MLPs when predicting Young’s modulus and mass-normalized modulus, because tensor factorizations exploit multilinear correlations to accurately infer unobserved entries from few, non-uniform observations.
